message:
Welcome to Version Confusion, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

? *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands*
 ? *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once youre ready just click start to begin
Hey guys im seeing an issue with the website loading slowly
Anyone else experaning this?
No all ok for me
No issue for me
What browser and version of it are you using Tanya?
Im using chrome and let me check the version
Let me test
Ok if it is V105.0 this is a recorded bug with a ticket in the backlog
Yeah it is V105.0
:star: Reproduces the incident
Should we not raise an incident for this? I know I can update the version and it will stop effecting me.
But could this not be effecting customers?
Had a look at the market share of people using this version it is only at 0.09%
Still not sure if it should not be an incidnt. @aoife.breen-options-it.com What do you think?
Seems fine for me too
:star: Attempt to replicate the issue
if this is a known issue and only affects an extremely small number of customers, I don't believe a critical incident is needed.
@bob  has there been any customer reports?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Sounds like a frontend perf bug—we’ve got those on the backlog

There is a ticket from two weeks ago.”

Title: Slow load times for users using V105.0 of chrome

Description: Users report frontend loading slowly; quick look suggests frontend issue on V105.0

Reporter: User

Components: OB-Frontend
@tanya  what would the impact be of updating the version? is it a disruptive fix?
:star: Reviewed changes
Gathering reports
@daniel  why has this not yet been deployed?
:star: Manage actions and timings
There are more important things to do atm
Even if it’s a small share, degradation trends can hide something bigger
Are there other checks we can complete to gather wider scope of the issue
Checking the impact of ugrading to never version
Front end looks fine right now
i can check the services to see if I can spot anything in logs
as does customer traffic
Oh—reports actually show ~20% of customers impacted
They’re seeing 3× longer load times, and there are some timeouts
Trend has been building for two weeks
Thank you for confirming Bob.
We’re down ~10% sales vs two-week average
If it’s slow loads + form delays, could be CheckoutService latency or payment authorisation retries
This is a bigger issue. #incident - Website slowness
*Incident Number INC100010:* - website slowness
:star: Incident record created
Really dont think we need an incident. As I said we have a bug already and really need to focus on delivery of new features.
Hi All, we are investigating the root cause of the slowness to determine if this has been a result of a build up of users with outdated website version, or a bigger issue. Criticality TBC
:star: Form a working theory
Keep me posted on this, I need to know the customer and business impact ASAP
@daniel - we are acknowledging the issue & determining if this is actually related to the bug.
@tanya  any update on the impact of deploying new version?
@bob  when have customer reports increased
Still checking all the plausible scenarios
They have slightly elevated in the past 8h
Understood. Could be AdService blocking render threads. Ads can cause slow load and timeouts
@shay have any changes been made in last 8h that could have caused greater impact here?
:star: Check changes done to the platform

https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/ZDQ1MTE3NWU5ZDYxNDA4MTkxZmM3ZGJmMGJhNmE1OTE

Those are all the changes we have made recently
Ad service doesn't look to show anything abnormal currently
Based on my assesment the new version will conflict with our current configurations. Not an option
Agree, i think that was jsut a theory
Thanks guys. I can see some checkout related errors
If it’s still ‘Chrome only’ but high %, that suggests version-range not just 105
@shay can you please double check impact of CHG89356 incase?
Sure
@tanya  would also like you to review - CHG89374 to investigate Shay's theory RE ads causing slowness
@bob can we reassure customers that we're aware of some website slowness & looking into it?
Doesn't look like the recent change is related to this. It's too recent ,and nothing in there could cause an issue
@daniel on a seperate note, please prioritise the version change in the next window.
I will put a post out about this
I will add it to the next available slot
Checking the change now
Website version is not an incident - expected
However in last 8h reports have increased
I have review the change, nothing stands out and service logs are clean

~20% of customers impacted
They’re seeing 3× longer load times, and there are some timeouts
Trend has been building for two weeks
Checking infra
:star: Qualify the problem
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
Do other web browsers work fine with all versions?
Do we need more people involved for this or we have under control?
According to reports, it seems to be just Chrome
@hamed  could you please have a look here incase we are missing something?
Checking: TLS/HTTP negotiation, headers, caching behavior, NGINX config/version changes, and any edge errors segmented by UA
Sure, do we know anything so far?
Need edge/web server validation. Browser-specific degradation with timeouts!
Under control for now
Could be frontend polyfills / bundler changes for older browsers

Website version is not an incident - expected
However in last 8h reports have increased 
~20% of customers impacted
They’re seeing 3× longer load times, and there are some timeouts
Trend has been building for two weeks
Chrome 105 shows longer TTFB than Chrome latest. That hints at server-side negotiation or proxy behavior
Can we include in customer comms to update their chrome version? @bob
Okay. Good find Tanya!
@maya  would you be able to check firewall logs here please?
I can. I will start drafting new comms in a few moments!
Sure. I will start checking right away
Found it: NGINX version update introduced compatibility issue with Chrome 105 and older
Requests from those user agents are delayed/dropped due to unsupported behavior (header/feature handling)
This explains: slow loads, intermittent timeouts, and why frontend looks ‘fine’
Great find, thank you @hamed
What change can we deploy to resolve
We have two options here : Rollback NGINX to prior stable version or config adjustment to restore compatibility
which is the safest option intraday?
@daniel  what are you most comfortable with
Option 1 is fast and quick, but it's a temp fix
Option 2 is permanent fix but might take a while!
I can do a rollback
it's quick and easy
Can we implement option 1 for now & work on option 2 in the background ? then deploy when ready & site traffic is lower?
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
I’ll prep a short note: ‘We identified a performance regression impacting some Chrome versions; mitigation underway
Thanks Bob!
Yeah, sounds good to me
Let's go with that. @shay can you please test following the change
I will work on the fix and do a rollback
:thumbsup:
Sure
Hey @aoife.breen-options-it.com I have just retried the website with V105.0 of chrome and it seems the fix worked
Glad we created an incident for this. Need to review our training on when to create an incident or a bug but great work team.
Sorry guys should have raised an incident when first discovered.
We are all learning Daniel. We can carry the learnings foward
Just checked and it looks all good now team
Lets continue monitoring this and we can have a great conversation during the PIR
Incident resolved temporarily until permanent fix implemented. Thanks!

Thank you for playing Version Confusion, We recommend that you take 5 minutes to go and get a drink or stretch your legs.
Once you are back we ask that you complete this retrospective and feedback form, just hit start when you are ready.
